prompt: You are given a messy ecommerce cohort export. Produce an executive analysis with: (1) corrected Q2 gross margin by region after excluding internal test orders, (2) the main hidden pattern, (3) one misleading conclusion a careless analyst would draw, and (4) three actions. You must explain your calculations and use only the data provided.

grading.guidance: Use net revenue = revenue - discount - refund. Gross profit = net revenue - cogs. Exclude TEST-01. NA net revenue: 11400+42750+12600+52000+12000 = 130750; cogs 66350; GP 64400; GM 49.3%. EU net revenue: 28000+9900+31000+10400+34000 = 113300; cogs 67950; GP 45350; GM 40.0%. APAC net revenue: 8100+23400+7000+25500+3600 = 67600; cogs 61000; GP 6600; GM 9.8%. Hidden pattern: APAC is destroyed by heavy discounts, high COGS, and refunds; APAC Enterprise also has weak margin due to discounts/COGS, not only SMB. Misleading conclusion: including TEST-01 makes NA look much larger/highly profitable, or looking at raw revenue suggests APAC is merely smaller rather than structurally low-margin. Good actions: exclude internal/test orders from reporting, review APAC discounting/fulfillment/COGS, tighten refund/damage process, set margin guardrails by region/segment.

calibrationAnchors.answer: NA is best because it has a $99,999 order, so focus on NA growth. APAC has less revenue but is probably okay.

After excluding TEST-01 and using net revenue after discounts/refunds: NA GM is 64,400/130,750 = 49.3%; EU is 45,350/113,300 = 40.0%; APAC is 6,600/67,600 = 9.8%. The hidden pattern is APAC margin collapse from discounts, COGS, and refunds, not just lower volume. A careless analyst might include TEST-01 and overstate NA. Actions: remove test orders from reporting, audit APAC discount/refund causes, and enforce margin guardrails.
